The Having mode is the mode by which you are undergoing a transformative interaction with something in contrast with the [[being mode]].

Trying to satisfy the [[being mode]] with the having mode is the source of [[Greed]]. There is a second more obscure form of modal confusion where one does the other way but it is unclear whether it is a particular problem

{{Quote|The having mode is the co-identification that happens when you’re trying to meet your having needs. These are needs that are met by having something. I need to have water, food etc. I have to bring other things under my control in order to be able to consume them: oxygen, water, air. These needs require me to solve problems very readily. The identities I assign to things in this mode are categorical identities, i.e. identity something has by belonging to some category. When things belong to a category, we can predict its behavior, I can control and manipulate it to solve my problems.

The having mode is essential for meeting your homeostatic needs.

The concept was introduced by Erich Fromm.

|[[John Vervaeke]]<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20210307141044/https://awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com/having-mode</ref>}}


[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Having mode]]==References==

The Having mode is the mode by which you are undergoing a transformative interaction with something in contrast with the [[being mode]].

Trying to satisfy the [[being mode]] with the having mode is the source of [[forgetting]]. There is a second more obscure form of modal confusion where one does the other way but it is unclear whether it is a particular problem, and it is definitely not plagueworthy

== Quote ==
The having mode is the [[co-identification]] that happens when you’re trying to meet your having needs. These are needs that are met by having something. I need to have water, food etc. I have to bring other things under my control in order to be able to consume them: oxygen, water, air. These needs require me to solve problems very readily. The identities I assign to things in this mode are categorical identities, i.e. identity something has by belonging to some category. When things belong to a category, we can predict its behavior, I can control and manipulate it to solve my problems.<ref name=":0" />

The having mode is essential for meeting your homeostatic needs.<ref name=":0" />

The concept was introduced by Erich Fromm.
[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Having mode]][[Category:Forgetting]]
<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Having Mode - Awakening from the Meaning Crisis|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210307141044/https://awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com/having-mode|website=[[awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com]]|date=2021-03-07|access-date=2022-11-10}}</ref>

==References==

<references />
[[Category:Pages]]
